instruction: Classify the security alert into a threat category.
baseline_prompt: You are a SOC alert triage analyst. Classify the security alert into a threat category. Classify into one of these categories: phishing, malware, brute_force, data_exfiltration, insider_threat, ddos.
source: Authored for TEI-Bench (Sonnet-generated, Sonnet-validated; kept only label-agreed examples).

train.query: Email security gateway flagged message with spoofed display name matching our CFO. Body text creates urgency about account suspension and directs recipient to verify identity through embedded link. URL analysis reveals typosquatting domain targeting our organization. Landing page requests full credentials and security questions.

Multiple workstations reporting encrypted files with .locked extension appended. Ransom note detected in user directories demanding cryptocurrency payment. Network share access patterns indicate lateral spread from initially compromised host in accounting department.

SSH daemon logs indicate systematic login failures targeting user 'sysadmin' from botnet IP addresses. Pattern shows dictionary-based credential testing with 50-80 attempts per minute sustained over 3 hours. No successful authentications recorded.

Unusual FTP session detected from internal file server to unknown external host 203.0.113.89. Session duration 4 hours with sustained outbound traffic rate of 50Mbps. Authentication logs show service account credentials were used. Files accessed include proprietary source code repositories.

Contractor account bthomas copied 500+ files containing product roadmaps and competitive analysis documents to a personal laptop, then immediately accessed LinkedIn and uploaded a resume indicating active job searching at competitor firms.

Alert: External-facing DNS server 192.168.50.12 experiencing query volume of 450,000 requests per second from distributed sources across 23 countries. Legitimate query rate typically averages 3,200/sec. Server CPU at 98%, memory exhausted. Majority of queries are malformed or requesting non-existent subdomains.

User forwarded suspicious shipping notification supposedly from major courier service. Email contains no actual tracking number but includes 'Verify Delivery Address' button. Link analysis shows redirection to credential input form hosted on compromised WordPress site. Similar campaign detected across 47 mailboxes in past hour.

SIEM correlation detected cryptominer process consuming 98% CPU resources across 15 servers in production environment. Unauthorized mining pool connections established to monero-pool.org. Initial infection vector traced to vulnerable Apache Struts instance.

RDP service on production server 10.45.2.19 recorded 2,341 failed authentication attempts from external IP 103.75.22.89 between 02:15 and 02:47 UTC. Multiple usernames attempted including administrator, admin, root, and service accounts.

Email gateway blocked outbound message containing 47 attachments (total 124MB) to external Gmail address. Attachments include database exports, API documentation, and customer contact lists. Sender attempted to bypass DLP controls by splitting archive into multiple parts.

Employee account jsmith accessed the HR salary database at 2:47 AM from their workstation, downloading 15,000 personnel records to an external USB drive. This user typically works 9-5 and has never accessed HR systems before.

SIEM correlation rule triggered: ICMP echo request storm detected from 2,400+ sources simultaneously targeting corporate gateway 198.51.100.1. Packet inspection reveals fragmented payloads characteristic of coordinated flood. Network latency increased 1200ms above normal. VPN concentrator failing health checks due to resource starvation.

Endpoint detection tool alerted on browser behavior after user interaction with email claiming expired security certificate. Email contained convincing branding from our VPN provider with urgent renewal instructions. Clicked link opened lookalike login portal at vpn-renewal-secure.com harvesting domain credentials from 3 users before being blocked.

Sandbox analysis of email attachment 'invoice_2847.exe' reveals keylogging capabilities, credential harvesting modules, and encrypted communication to external IP addresses. Static analysis shows packing with UPX and anti-debugging functions embedded in the code.

Authentication logs from webmail server show 847 consecutive login failures for user account 'jdavis@company.com' originating from IP 185.220.101.47 over a 12-minute window. Account lockout policy triggered after threshold exceeded.

test.query: Cloud access security broker flagged abnormal Box.com sync activity. User jennifer.martinez uploaded 2,847 documents totaling 5.1GB from Engineering/Patents directory to personal cloud storage account. Upload pattern inconsistent with normal usage baseline for this employee.

Marketing employee taccess attempted to view proprietary source code repositories for engineering projects unrelated to their role, making 47 failed access attempts across different systems within a 3-hour window.

CloudFront distribution alert: Origin server 10.10.10.50 receiving 1.2 million simultaneous connection attempts. Pattern analysis indicates Layer 7 attack using randomized user agents and slowly-transmitted POST requests designed to exhaust application threads. Cache hit ratio dropped to 4%. Customer portal experiencing complete outage.

SIEM correlation detected anomalous email from external sender impersonating company's legal counsel requesting confidential case file access. Message bypassed initial filters using zero-point font obfuscation. Embedded link leads to fake Office365 authentication page collecting usernames and passwords from legal team members.

USB device auto-run initiated worm propagation affecting 23 endpoints in manufacturing facility. Self-replicating executable copying itself to network shares and removable media while dropping info-stealer component targeting browser credentials and cryptocurrency wallets.

VPN gateway logs reveal 456 authentication failures for multiple domain accounts originating from IP range 194.165.16.0/24. Failed attempts span 18 different usernames with common passwords being tested sequentially.

Firewall logs show employee workstation transmitted 12,000 files to personal Dropbox account within 15 minutes. Total transfer size 8.2GB including contents from shared drives containing customer records and financial spreadsheets. Activity occurred during user's last day of employment.

System administrator account dparker created five new privileged accounts with naming patterns similar to service accounts, granted them domain admin rights, and accessed multiple executives' email folders over a weekend period.

Network monitoring detected abnormal traffic spike from 847 unique source IPs targeting web server 10.45.23.101 on port 443. Traffic volume exceeded 15 Gbps, representing a 2300% increase from baseline. Server response times degraded to timeout levels. SYN packets dominate the traffic pattern with minimal completion of TCP handshakes.

Web proxy logs show user clicked link in email purporting to be from HR benefits portal. Destination site cloned our legitimate benefits provider interface with nearly identical branding. Site hosted on newly registered domain benefits-verify-portal.net. Form fields capture username, password, and MFA token.

Endpoint detection triggered on DESKTOP-8472 showing suspicious executable 'svchost32.exe' running from %TEMP% directory with process injection behavior and attempting to modify registry run keys for persistence. File hash matches known Emotet variant signatures.
Office 365 tenant protection flagged anomalous sign-in activity: 1,247 password validation failures for executive accounts from TOR exit nodes across 7 geographic regions within 20-minute timeframe.

DNS queries reveal workstation WS-2847 establishing connection to file-sharing service WeTransfer. Network monitoring captured 3.4GB upload to external recipient outside organization. Source files originated from HR shared folder containing employee personal information and salary data.

Privileged admin credentials for user mwilliams were used to disable audit logging on three file servers, followed by access to confidential merger documents in the legal department share. User submitted resignation two days prior.

Firewall logs show UDP flood targeting gaming servers in DMZ subnet 172.30.0.0/24. Inbound packet rate sustained at 8.5 million packets per second from spoofed source addresses. Bandwidth consumption at 23 Gbps. Game service authentication system non-responsive. Edge routers approaching maximum packet processing capacity.

User reported suspicious email from what appeared to be IT department requesting password reset via external link. Email header analysis shows sender domain microsoftt-support.com with recent registration date. Link redirects through multiple shortened URLs before landing on a login page mimicking our corporate SSO portal. Page source contains credential harvesting JavaScript.

Host-based intrusion prevention system blocked file-less attack leveraging PowerShell Empire framework. Malicious script injected into memory space of legitimate Windows process attempting to disable antivirus services and establish reverse shell connection.

Database server access logs show repetitive connection attempts using invalid credentials. Source 91.203.5.118 attempted to authenticate with 'sa' account 2,890 times in 45-minute period before firewall rule blocked traffic.
